{"key_event":"Congo wars and fragmented sovereignty","reasoning":"The First and Second Congo Wars shattered the already hollow Mobutu-era state, divided sovereignty among rebels and foreign armies, and left Kinshasa with only partial administrative and coercive reach despite the 2003 transitional government. Civil society retained some capacity through churches, NGOs, human-rights groups, and peace-process participation, but mass displacement, militia rule, ethnic polarization, and war-economy predation reduced its effective ability to organize and constrain power.","society_change":-0.4,"society_power":3.2,"state_change":-0.6,"state_power":2.5}
